text: Two months post-operation, the presacral cavity is clean with visible granulation tissue, ready for closure. Under general anesthesia, the transanal platform is installed, revealing the blind loop, afferent loop, and anastomotic defect. Gauze is inserted into the afferent loop to prevent accidental closure during suturing. The cavity shows clean granulation tissue, but increased fibrosis necessitates mobilization of the rectal cuff from surrounding structures.

The surgeon inserts a six to eight French suction drain into the old abscess cavity, positioning it one to two centimeters distal to the anastomotic defect, immediately posterior to the rectal wall. The drain is then secured with a fast-resorbable Ficryl-III-Zero suture to prevent the formation of a new abscess.

The transanal platform is removed and repositioned to keep the suction drain outside its lumen. The drain is visibly fixated outside the platform. The surgeon closes the cavity using separate interrupted full-thickness Ficryl-II-Zero sutures. Gas is observed in the afferent loop, preventing its closure during the suturing process. After the defect is fully closed, the gas will be removed to confirm the afferent loop's patency.
